content: We can frame Chain of Thought (CoT) reasoning not just as a static one-off prompting technique, but as a *temporary, sequentially built active working memory* for active learning AI agents, where agents selectively retain high-impact CoT traces into long-term agent memory to improve future reasoning on novel tasks. This creates a closed loop where active learning's query for uncertain, high-value data extends to querying which past CoT traces are worth retaining, rather than storing all reasoning history.
